hero.heading: Peptide Dosing & Safety Guide
hero.subtitle: Standardized reconstitution and dosing protocols for commonly used clinical peptides. Accurate measurement using U-100 insulin syringes with proper storage guidelines.

overview.heading: How to Use This Guide
overview.body: This comprehensive dosing resource covers reconstitution, measurement conversions, and administration protocols for common clinical peptides using U-100 insulin syringes (100 units = 1.0 mL). Always verify all calculations independently before administration.

safetyPrinciples.heading: Key Safety Principles
items: Always double-check your math, reconstitution calculations, and dosing — even when using AI tools or calculators. Verify every step yourself to ensure safety and accuracy.
Do not shake vials. Inject diluent slowly down the vial wall and gently swirl until dissolved.
Store mixed vials in the refrigerator (2–8°C) unless instructed otherwise. Label with date mixed and concentration.
Rotate injection sites to prevent tissue damage or lipohypertrophy. Use new sterile equipment for each injection.
Stop and contact your clinician immediately if experiencing severe symptoms: chest pain, fainting, severe headache, or unusual swelling.

steps.title: Prepare Workspace
steps.desc: Clean your workspace and wash hands thoroughly. Gather all supplies: vial, bacteriostatic water, alcohol swabs, and insulin syringe.

steps.title: Sterilize Vials
steps.desc: Swab vial tops with alcohol and allow to dry completely before insertion.

steps.title: Draw Diluent
steps.desc: Draw the appropriate volume of bacteriostatic water into the syringe.

steps.title: Inject Slowly
steps.desc: Inject slowly down the vial wall — not directly into the powder — to avoid denaturing the peptide.

steps.title: Swirl Gently
steps.desc: Gently swirl the vial until fully dissolved. Never shake.

steps.title: Label & Store
steps.desc: Label the vial with the date mixed and final concentration. Refrigerate immediately.

conversionRule.heading: U-100 Insulin Syringe Conversion
conversionRule.body: U-100 insulin syringes are calibrated so that 100 units = 1.0 mL and 10 units = 0.1 mL. Each peptide's specific unit conversion depends on the final concentration achieved during reconstitution.

peptides.name: Retatrutide
peptides.notes: Titrate slowly based on tolerability. GLP-1/GIP/glucagon triple agonist. Monitor for GI side effects especially during titration.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 28 days.

peptides.name: Tirzepatide (TIRZ)
peptides.notes: Dual GLP-1/GIP agonist. Monitor for nausea; hold or reduce dose if GI symptoms are severe. Titrate every 4 weeks as tolerated.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 28 days.

peptides.name: Semaglutide (SEMA)
peptides.notes: GLP-1 receptor agonist. Start low and titrate slowly. Dose escalation typically every 4 weeks.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 28 days.

peptides.name: AOD-9604
peptides.notes: Fragment of human growth hormone targeting fat metabolism. Often dosed in the morning on an empty stomach.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 30 days.

peptides.name: BPC-157
peptides.notes: Supports musculoskeletal and gastrointestinal healing. Can be dosed subcutaneously or intramuscularly near the injury site.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 30 days.

peptides.name: TB-500 (Thymosin Beta-4)
peptides.notes: Promotes tissue repair, reduces inflammation, and improves flexibility. Often combined with BPC-157 for synergistic effect.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 30 days.

peptides.name: GHK-Cu (Copper Peptide)
peptides.notes: Supports collagen synthesis, wound healing, and skin regeneration. May be used topically or subcutaneously.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 30 days.

peptides.name: CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin
peptides.notes: Both compounds share equal concentration at 1:1 ratio. Best dosed before sleep to align with natural GH pulse. Avoid after high-carbohydrate meals.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 30 days.

peptides.name: Ipamorelin
peptides.notes: Selective GHRP with minimal effect on cortisol or prolactin. Typically dosed 2–3× daily or before bed.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 30 days.

peptides.name: Sermorelin
peptides.notes: GHRH analogue. Best dosed before sleep. Effects build over weeks — typically evaluated at 3–6 months.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 30 days.

peptides.name: Tesamorelin
peptides.notes: FDA-approved GHRH analogue. Reduces visceral fat. Monitor IGF-1 levels during treatment.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 30 days.

peptides.name: NAD+
peptides.notes: Infuse slowly. Flush reactions (warmth, chest tightness) are common — slow infusion rate if they occur. Often given IV push or slow IV drip.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 14 days.

peptides.name: MOTS-c
peptides.notes: Mitochondrial-derived peptide supporting metabolic homeostasis and insulin sensitivity. Often given 2–3× weekly.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 30 days.

peptides.name: Epithalon
peptides.notes: Telomere-regulating tetrapeptide. Typical courses run 10–20 days, 1–2× per year. Often dosed in the evening.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 30 days.

peptides.name: KPV
peptides.notes: Anti-inflammatory tripeptide. Effective for gut inflammation (IBD, colitis). Can be administered subcutaneously or orally (compounded capsule).
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 30 days.

peptides.name: LL-37
peptides.notes: Human cathelicidin antimicrobial peptide. Supports innate immunity and wound healing. Monitor injection site for redness.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 21 days.

peptides.name: ARA-290
peptides.notes: Erythropoietin-derived peptide with anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties. Used for neuropathic pain and inflammation.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 30 days.

peptides.name: PT-141 (Bremelanotide)
peptides.notes: Dose 30–45 min before activity. Common side effects include nausea and flushing. Do not exceed one dose per 72 hours.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 30 days.

peptides.name: Selank
peptides.notes: Anxiolytic nootropic with BDNF-stimulating properties. Intranasal or subcutaneous. Typically cycled 10–14 days on, 1–2 weeks off.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 30 days.

peptides.name: Semax
peptides.notes: ACTH-derived nootropic. Enhances BDNF, NGF, and cognitive performance. Intranasal administration preferred. Cycle similar to Selank.
peptides.storage: Refrigerate after reconstitution. Use within 30 days.

disclaimer.heading: Important Medical Disclaimer
disclaimer.body: This guide is provided for educational and reference purposes only. All protocols are standardized examples and cannot substitute for individualized medical advice from a licensed clinician. Patient-specific protocols may differ from the examples provided. Always verify calculations independently. PeptidePure™ products are intended exclusively for use by licensed healthcare professionals and qualified researchers. Administration of these compounds should only occur under appropriate clinical supervision.
